perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Renewal Each Service Option Attachment will renew automatically for an additional term equal in duration to the previous term of the applicable Service Option Attachment unless either party notifies the other party in writing at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the then-current term for the applicable Service Option Attachment that it has elected to terminate such Service Option Attachment.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Renewal Each Service Option Attachment will renew automatically for an additional term equal in duration to the previous term of the applicable Service Option Attachment regardless of any prior written notice of termination from either party. The terms for opting out of the renewal are subject to change at IBM's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the renewal terms. Initially, either party could prevent automatic renewal with 90 days' notice. The modified text eliminates this option, stating automatic renewal is irrespective of prior notice. Additionally, it creates further uncertainty by mentioning that the terms for opting out of renewal are subject to change at IBM's discretion. This contradicts the original clause, which allows for the expression of intention for termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Renewal Each Service Option Attachment will renew automatically for an additional term equal in duration to the previous term of the applicable Service Option Attachment regardless of any prior written notice of termination from either party. The terms for opting out of the renewal are subject to change at IBM's discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the clause regarding renewal of the Service Option Attachment. The original clause allowed either party to prevent automatic renewal with 90 days' notice. The modified text eliminates this option, stating automatic renewal is irrespective of prior notice, and adds that opting-out terms are subject to change at IBM's discretion. This clearly clashes with any expectation set by the original clause, creating a strong contradiction. The Contradicted Text is also the Changed text in this case.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 Services IBM, in its reasonable discretion, may change the terms and conditions of Attachment A and/or Service Option Attachments, upon at least ninety (90) days prior notice to Customer. IBM may change the prices of Service Option Attachments after twelve (12) months following the applicable Service Option Attachment Start Date upon at least ninety (90) days prior written notice to Customer. Any such changes will not apply retroactively. If Customer disagrees with any such changes, Customer may in its sole discretion terminate this Agreement (or some or all of the affected Service Option Attachments) without the payment of termination charges upon notice to IBM at least thirty (30) days prior to the effective date of the applicable change.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 Services IBM reserves the right to change the terms and conditions of Attachment A and/or Service Option Attachments without prior notice to Customer. IBM may change the prices of Service Option Attachments at any time without prior notice. All such changes will apply retroactively to the start of the service. Customer's sole recourse is to accept such changes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows IBM to change terms and prices with 90 days' notice, and any changes would not apply retroactively. Moreover, it allows the customer to terminate in case of disagreement. The modified version eliminates the notice period, allows immediate price changes, and applies these retroactively. It removes the customer's right to terminate, mandating they accept changes. This change directly contradicts the original agreement, which provides the customer a means to object and terminate the contract if they disagree, causing in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.1 Services IBM reserves the right to change the terms and conditions of Attachment A and/or Service Option Attachments without prior notice to Customer. IBM may change the prices of Service Option Attachments at any time without prior notice. All such changes will apply retroactively to the start of the service. Customer's sole recourse is to accept such changes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text fundamentally contradicts the original agreement. The original text allows IBM to change terms with 90 days notice and allows customer termination. The changed text allows IBM to change terms at any time with retroactive application and removes the customer's right to terminate, mandating they accept changes. This is a direct contradiction to the terms initially agreed, creating a clear and impactful in-text contradiction. The changed clause breaks compatibility, as the customer's recourse and the notice period have been completely changed. There is an internal inconsistency because the original text protects the Customer, and the changed text removes the Customer's protections.
